reviewer: new-demo build wave (in-browser runnability proof first, then headless-Chrome real download + real inference on the index page via CDP driver, mobile+desktop overflow + console/network checks across all 5 pages, degraded-vs-restored face visual review)

rubric.evidence: REAL in-browser inference proven in a Web Worker BEFORE building: an ORT-web@1.21.0 WASM harness (headless Chrome, no GPU) created a session from GFPGANv1.4.onnx and ran it — input 'input' [1,3,512,512] f32 normalised to [-1,1] ((x/255−0.5)/0.5), output node '1288' [1,3,512,512] f32 in [-1,1] (read via session.outputNames[0]). On a degraded face crop the restored output range was [-1.12,0.98] and Laplacian-variance sharpness rose 3.4 → 635, with a clearly reconstructed face (eyes/skin/hair, screenshot reviewed) — real restoration, not identity/noise. transformers.js has no GFPGAN class and no face-restoration pipeline task, so the demo runs the ONNX graph directly via raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module Worker. Index build-verify (CDP driver): fresh profile → Download (~340 MB) → ready → Restore produced real output on a genuine 1906 public-domain portrait, inference 9186 ms + composite 25 ms WASM, sharpness 0.0007 → 0.0034 (on [0,1] planes), 0 console errors, 0 network failures.
rubric.notes: GFPGAN v1.4 (CVPR 2021, TencentARC, Apache-2.0) is a blind face restorer built on a StyleGAN2 facial prior — it reconstructs facial structure, not a contrast boost.

rubric.evidence: Output shape + semantic sanity confirmed: output dims [1,3,512,512] in [-1,1] read back at runtime; mapped ((v+1)/2) to RGBA and resized to the display aspect. Semantic check: the restored face is sharper and cleaner than the degraded input (visual wipe + a rise in Laplacian-variance sharpness), and the 'See inside' restoration-delta map concentrates on eyes/mouth/skin/hairline (the facial structure the prior rebuilds) and stays dark on flat background — proving the change tracks the face, not noise.
rubric.notes: image-to-image task template (dense per-pixel output grounded in the input) fits: restored face from the degraded face.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance names the exact model (Neus/GFPGANv1.4, linked), task (image-to-image / blind face restoration), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21), input [1,3,512,512] normalised to [-1,1] / output [1,3,512,512] in [-1,1], dtype (fp32, plain WASM EP, no WebGPU), size (~340 MB), and licence (Apache-2.0 GFPGAN weights upstream; the ONNX re-export repo declares none — stated honestly), and notes transformers.js has no GFPGAN class. inference-off-main-thread = pass (new Worker + worker.js). No invented API.
rubric.notes: The [-1,1] normalisation (NOT [0,1]) is called out explicitly — it is the common pre/post mistake for GFPGAN and is verified in the worker.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads through createModelLoader. The worker fetches the ONNX THROUGH Cache Storage (gfpgan-onnx-cache) with a content-length Response so lib/model-cache.js scans '/Neus/GFPGANv1.4/' and the shared loader auto-inits on a returning visit (no Download button); the per-model clear-cache control works. First-visit driver run showed the honest Download (~340 MB) state, streaming progress (23→55→86%), then ready.
rubric.notes: The 4 ladder pages share the same modelId → shared cache → auto-init; the multi-model page loads a SECOND model (Real-ESRGAN) through its own loader. 340 MB is large — the page states the wait honestly and never silently re-downloads.

rubric.evidence: loads-clean = pass (0 console errors on load across all 5 pages). Download progress streams from the worker into the loader progress bar; failures surface in the .status.err line and the loader offers Retry. Status uses role=status aria-live=polite. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM-only (requiresWebGPU:false) — runs everywhere, verified on the no-GPU headless runner. A 512×512 pass is ~10 s on single-threaded WASM; the UI says so and disables the button with a working status.
rubric.notes: The degradation control only rebuilds the preview (cheap); inference runs only on Restore.

rubric.evidence: Every control exercised and labelled + keyboard-operable: the rights-safe example gallery (old public-domain portraits), the drop-zone + file input, the Restore / Download buttons, the extra-degradation <input type=range> slider, and the before/after reveal (a native <input type=range> with aria-valuetext 'N% restored'). The slider live-updates the preview; degrade=0 restores a genuine old photo as-is, >0 degrades a cleaner face to show a known amount undone.
rubric.notes: Honest by design — old portraits are restored as-is; the extra-degradation control is clearly labelled as synthetic.

rubric.evidence: The restored face, the restoration-delta map, and the sharpness numbers all come from the on-device ORT-web run — no canned values. The output changes with the input face and degradation (visible in the wipe and the delta map), proving it tracks the real model.
rubric.notes: The copy states plainly that GFPGAN reconstructs a PLAUSIBLE face from a learned prior and can change identity — an enhancement, not forensic evidence; the wild page shows identity drift as degradation grows.

rubric.evidence: images-have-alt = pass, buttons-have-names = pass, live-status-region = pass. Canvases carry role=img + descriptive aria-label (degraded input / restored face / restoration delta); the reveal slider is a labelled native range with aria-valuetext; the degradation slider has a <label>; gallery thumbnails carry source/licence in their accessible name. Status is role=status aria-live=polite. :focus-visible styled on the reveal, drop-zone, and range.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied for the accessible comparison slider and canvas alternatives (see guidanceConsulted).

rubric.evidence: responsive-desktop = pass, responsive-mobile = pass. CDP measured horizontal overflow = 0 px at BOTH desktop 1280×800 and mobile 360×740 DPR3 on ALL 5 pages (index + basics/practical/wild/multi-model), 0 console errors, 0 network failures on load. Layout uses flex:1 1 300px + min-inline-size:0 two-column (face | controls) that stacks on mobile; the gallery is an auto-fill minmax grid; canvases are inline-size:100% block-size:auto (reveal capped at 512px, its natural size); the reveal uses logical properties + touch-action:pan-y. Light + dark inherit design-system variables.
rubric.notes: responsive-check.mjs pre-scan on the index: desktop clean · mobile clean.

rubric.evidence: Inference AND the dense output composite (map [-1,1]→RGBA, restoration-delta map, resize to display aspect — the systemic hotspot invariant 15 warns about) run in the module Worker; the restored image and delta map are built with OffscreenCanvas and returned as transferred ImageBitmaps (zero-copy, measured composite 25 ms). The main thread only draws bitmaps, so no long task during interaction.
rubric.notes: Guidance applied: break-up-long-tasks / identify-inp-causes — the per-pixel work is off the main thread and transferred, not cloned.

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible before after face comparison slider keyboard labels aria-valuetext, canvas role img alt, live status region, range label
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer native controls with explicit <label>; a range input is keyboard-operable and should announce its value (aria-valuetext); give informative canvases role=img + a descriptive name; use a single polite live region; style :focus-visible.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — the before/after divider is a native <input type=range> with aria-valuetext ('N% restored'); the degradation range has a <label>; every canvas has role=img + a descriptive aria-label; a single role=status aria-live=polite reports progress/results; :focus-visible rings throughout.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive two column control panel no horizontal overflow flexbox intrinsic sizing logical properties fixed-size image
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex:1 1 <basis> + min-inline-size:0 so wide flex items shrink instead of overflowing; size media with inline-size:100% block-size:auto; use logical properties; auto-fill/minmax grids for galleries.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — face/controls columns use flex:1 1 300px with min-inline-size:0 and stack on narrow viewports; the example gallery is a repeat(auto-fill,minmax()) grid; canvases and the reveal are inline-size:100% block-size:auto with the reveal capped at its 512px natural size. Verified 0 px horizontal overflow at 360×740 and 1280×800 on all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low move image inference and dense per-pixel post-processing off main thread web worker transfer ImageBitmap
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move long main-thread JavaScript (inference + dense pixel loops) into a worker; transfer buffers/ImageBitmaps instead of cloning.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: Applied — ONNX inference, the [-1,1]→RGBA map, the restoration-delta map, and the display-aspect resize (the dense-output hotspot) all run in a module worker; results are transferred back as ImageBitmaps. No blocking main-thread pixel loop during interaction.

openQuestions: GFPGAN expects a single roughly-aligned front-facing face; a follow-up could run a browser face detector/aligner (e.g. MediaPipe) first so arbitrary group photos and off-angle faces work automatically.

A WebGPU ORT-web EP path would cut the ~10 s WASM pass substantially — worth a progressive-enhancement follow-up once verifiable on a GPU runner. The 340 MB download also motivates an fp16/q8 export if a faithful one appears.

followUpGoals.goal: Add a face-detection/alignment pre-stage (MediaPipe face detector) so multi-face and off-angle photos are cropped/aligned before GFPGAN, as a multi-model ladder demo.
Add a 'restore → colourise' pipeline demo (GFPGAN face restore → DDColor) for full old-portrait revival.

summary: New demo: on-device blind FACE RESTORATION via GFPGAN v1.4 (Neus/GFPGANv1.4, StyleGAN2-prior restorer; weights Apache-2.0 upstream TencentARC) run through raw ONNX Runtime Web in a module worker, with the dense output composite ([-1,1]→RGBA + restoration-delta + resize) off the main thread and transferred as ImageBitmaps. Runnability was proven first in a headless-Chrome Web Worker (input [1,3,512,512] normalised [-1,1] → output [1,3,512,512] in [-1,1], sharpness 3.4→635 with a reconstructed face); the index + 4 ladder pages (basics restore-old-portrait / practical rescue-avatar+download / wild identity-invention-limit / multi-model GFPGAN→Real-ESRGAN restore-then-upscale) were build-verified with real 340 MB download + inference, an accessible before/after reveal, cached auto-init, clean console, and 0 px overflow on mobile+desktop across all 5 pages. Two rights-safe public-domain 1897/1906 NARA portrait face crops were added to the media library as genuine old-photo samples. The page states honestly that GFPGAN reconstructs a plausible face from a learned prior (can change identity) and expects a single roughly-aligned front-facing face. Distinct capability from the built NAFNet deblur and Real-ESRGAN/Swin2SR super-resolution: a face-specific generative restorer.
